hero.art_prompt: Priya, a 24 year old Indian commerce graduate woman in smart-casual, happily surprised at a laptop showing an affordable M.Com fee, bright hopeful mood, tidy home.

panels.narration: Priya compared M.Com fees across many options.
panels.dialogue: Why is a master's so costly?
panels.img_prompt: Priya, 24, at a desk comparing several high fee figures in a notebook, concerned and focused, tidy home.

panels.narration: Most good options ran into lakhs.
panels.dialogue: Is further study only for the rich?
panels.img_prompt: Priya looking discouraged at a laptop showing an expensive fee, chin in hand, soft disappointed expression, modest room.

panels.narration: Then she found a recognised M.Com at ₹45,000 a year.
panels.img_prompt: Priya's eyes widening with hope at a laptop showing a clear, affordable, recognised M.Com fee, warm glow of relief.

panels.narration: She enrolled on a 0% EMI she could manage.
panels.img_prompt: Priya smiling confidently holding a blank enrollment confirmation at home, calm and assured, warm hopeful light. No cash or money shown.

turn.title: Why This M.Com Fee Is A Steal
points: Just ₹90,000 for the full 2-year M.Com (₹45,000/year). A recognised master's at a fair, honest price.
Pay upfront and get a discount, or split it into easy 0% interest EMI.
0% interest EMI from about ₹3,750/month, so there is no heavy lump sum.
Only ₹2,500 one-time registration. Clear fees, no hidden charges.

testimonials.name: Priya Pillai
testimonials.quote: I compared everywhere. A UGC-entitled M.Com at ₹45,000 a year was the clear, honest choice.

testimonials.name: Karan Das
testimonials.quote: The 0% EMI meant no lump sum. I pay a small amount each month and my degree keeps going.

testimonials.name: Nisha Verma
testimonials.quote: We paid upfront and got a clear discount. A recognised master's at this price felt fair.

testimonials.name: Vikram Rao
testimonials.quote: I finished my M.Com without a heavy loan. The monthly plan fit my budget perfectly.

facts.v: 0% interest EMI from about ₹3,750/month, or an education loan
